Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First list the activities Receive Order RO Check Availability CA Reserve Items RI Pick Items PI Quality Check QC Pack Order PO Generate Shipping Label GSL Dispatch D Invoice Customer IC Record Payment RP

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: We need to infer constraints from the scenario description Lets think about typical process logic

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The description does not mention loops explicitly Possibly each activity occurs once per order But the scenario does not specify rework So we assume each activity occurs exactly once per case

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We assume a simple sequential process RO to CA to RI to PI to QC to PO to GSL to D to IC to RP

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Invoicing and payment might occur in parallel or after dispatch Typically Invoice Customer might be generated after dispatch or before not sure We will assume invoice is generated after dispatch

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For always before we can define the precedence constraints CA after RO RI after CA PI after RI QC after PI PO after QC GSL after PO D after GSL IC after D RP after IC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For always after this is the opposite of always before So we can define direct successor constraints RO to CA CA to RI RI to PI PI to QC QC to PO PO to GSL GSL to D D to IC IC to RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Never together Activities that cannot both occur in the same case Possibly none since in a normal flow all occur So I will set never together to empty set

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Activity frequency We assume each activity occurs exactly once So for each activity the allowed occurrence count is 1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly follows In a strict sequence each activity is directly followed by the next So directly follows is RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: In always after I had PO SL by mistake Correct is PO GSL

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check if any other constraints are needed The process might have branches if availability check fails but the scenario does not mention cancellation So we assume a happy path and go with the above
